caption: William Beckford as a Child, c.1764
description: Portrait of the young Beckford, heir to one of the greatest fortunes in England.

caption: William Beckford, c.1781
description: Full-length portrait painted when Beckford was twenty-one, shortly before he wrote Vathek during a three-day fever.

caption: William Beckford, c.1795
description: Portrait of the author of Vathek, aged around thirty-five — traveller, collector, and builder of Fonthill Abbey.

caption: William Beckford on Horseback
description: Sketch of Beckford in later life, renowned as an exceptionally fine horseman.

caption: William Beckford on his Deathbed, 1844
description: Miniature portrait in an elaborate gilt frame. Beckford died at his home on Lansdown Crescent, Bath, aged 84.

caption: Fonthill Abbey, West and North Fronts
description: Engraving of the great gothic pile against a dramatic sky. Fonthill Abbey was the largest private house ever built in England.

caption: Cross-Section of Fonthill Abbey Tower
description: Architectural engraving revealing the extraordinary internal structure of Fonthill's central tower, which soared to over 270 feet.

caption: Plan of the Principal Story, Fonthill Abbey, 1823
description: From John Rutter's survey. The cruciform plan shows the Grand Drawing Room, galleries, oratory, and the vast octagon beneath the tower.

caption: Map of the Fonthill Domain, 1823
description: Surveyed and planned for John Rutter's 'History and Description of Fonthill Abbey', showing the vast walled estate.

caption: King Edward's Gallery, Fonthill Abbey
description: Coloured aquatint showing the richly furnished long gallery hung with paintings and tapestries.

caption: The Great Drawing Room, Fonthill Abbey
description: Engraving of the Great Drawing Room at Fonthill Abbey, showing Beckford's remarkable collection of paintings, objets d'art, and furnishings.

caption: The Vaulted Corridor, Fonthill Abbey
description: Coloured aquatint of one of the great internal galleries, with fan-vaulted ceiling and crimson-hung walls stretching into the distance.

caption: St Michael's Gallery, Fonthill Abbey
description: Coloured aquatint showing the oratory with its soaring gothic windows and view over the Wiltshire landscape.

caption: A Fête at Fonthill Abbey, c.1822
description: Scene in the fountain court during one of Beckford's legendary entertainments.

caption: The Ruins of Fonthill Abbey after the Tower Collapse, 1825
description: The great tower fell in December 1825, two years after Beckford sold the estate. The ruins were largely demolished.

caption: Beckford's Tower, Bath
description: The tower Beckford built on Lansdown Hill, Bath — his second great architectural project after Fonthill Abbey collapsed.

caption: The Crimson Drawing Room, Lansdown Tower, Bath
description: Coloured illustration of the Crimson Drawing Room at Lansdown Tower, showing Beckford's richly decorated Bath retreat.

caption: The Tunnel at Lansdown Tower, Bath
description: Drawing of the subterranean passage that connected Beckford's Lansdown gardens to the hillside above.
